summary: Make the first screen explain identity, situation, priority problem, and next action within ten seconds
acceptance_criteria: First screen shows who the player is, current season/date, current strategic problem, and one recommended next action without opening extra panels.
Player guidance is elevated from generic checklist into a concise objective/action card tied to selected district and existing command previews.
The UI must answer: what is happening, why it matters, what I can do, what it costs, and what happens if ignored.
No irreversible action is submitted without preview and confirmation; no player-only command path is introduced.
No raw debug labels, prototype labels, milestone names, internal IDs, state hash, raw reason code, or test-only labels appear in player mode.
notes: This is an M7 product-owner REQUEST_CHANGES recovery task before formal content lock can be reconsidered.
M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and must not be rewritten as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; do not create manual battle implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.
UI must use read models and existing GameCommand/query boundaries; do not mutate authoritative WorldState from React, Pixi, or renderer.
Pixi/map presentation is render-cache-only and must remain rebuildable from read models.
No server, multiplayer, telemetry, account, cloud-save implementation, release, commercial, branding, production-art, or paid-service scope is authorized.
All player-visible text must go through existing i18n boundaries.
